source.title: SPACIER: on-demand polymer design with fully automated all-atom classical molecular dynamics integrated into machine learning pipelines

initial_request.research_problem: On-demand polymer design is hindered by data scarcity and the difficulty of automating all-atom MD simulations for property prediction.
initial_request.target_object: polymers with desired properties (e.g., high refractive index, high Abbe number)
initial_request.known_context: Existing polymer databases like PoLyInfo have limited data (e.g., below 30 samples for thermal conductivity). ML predictors are interpolative and cannot extrapolate to novel polymers. Automating all-atom MD simulations for polymers faces technical barriers.
constraints: Polymers must be synthesizable in one step from commercially available monomers.
Virtual library limited to 101,487 candidates from SMiPoly.
input_data: Experimental data from PoLyInfo and other sources for calibration.
Virtual polymer library generated by SMiPoly.
quantifiable_goals: Detect polymers in target property regions within 20-30 BO cycles.
Cross empirical Pareto frontier for refractive index and Abbe number.
Achieve good agreement between experimental and calculated properties (e.g., refractive index within 0.01, Abbe number within 0.0).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Polymer design is limited by scarce experimental data and interpolative ML predictors.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Lack of data and inability of ML to extrapolate to novel polymers.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Data scarcity is a major challenge in data-driven polymer research.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Identify data scarcity as a key problem.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Data scarcity is a major challenge; existing databases have very limited polymer data.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to address technical barriers in automating MD simulations.

evidence.quote_or_span: A major challenge in data-driven materials research is the lack of data resources.
the scarcity of data on polymeric materials is remarkable.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to specify BO method and polymer representation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pool-based BO with GP surrogate and force-field kernel mean descriptor can effectively search polymer space.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use pool-based BO with GP and force-field kernel mean descriptor.
evolution_trajectory.observation: With a given library of virtual polymers, a pool-based BO was applied to identify promising candidates.

evidence.quote_or_span: With a given library of virtual polymers generated as described later, a pool-based BO was applied to identify promising candidates with the desired properties.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No virtual library of synthesizable polymers exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a diverse set of candidate polymers for BO.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SMiPoly can generate a large virtual library of one-step synthesizable polymers.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use SMiPoly with 22 reaction rules to generate virtual polymers.
evolution_trajectory.observation: SMiPoly generated a virtual polymer library.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Set up multi-objective BO acquisition functions.

evidence.quote_or_span: SMiPoly is a virtual polymer generator that implements 22 polymerization reaction rules

evolution_trajectory.state_before: RadonPy properties may deviate from experiments.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Quantify systematic biases in Cp and refractive index.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cp overestimates due to missing quantum effects; refractive index underestimates due to density underestimation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare RadonPy-calculated Cp and refractive index with experimental data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Cp overestimates experimental values; refractive index underestimates.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Fit linear calibration models to correct biases.

evidence.quote_or_span: the calculated values for $C_{p}$ overestimate the experimental values because of the absence of quantum effects in classical MD simulations
The refractive index calculated using RadonPy slightly underestimates the experimental values, likely owing to an underestimation of the density

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Systematic biases exist in Cp and refractive index.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to correct biases to improve BO predictions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Linear calibration models can correct systematic biases.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Fit linear models to experimental values for Cp and refractive index.
evolution_trajectory.observation: MAE for Cp: 167.53, R²: 0.61; MAE for refractive index: 0.02, R²: 0.92.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply BO with calibrated models to illustrative example.

evidence.quote_or_span: The linear models were fitted to the experimental values to correct for these systematic biases
The mean absolute errors were 167.53 and 0.02, and the coefficients of determination were 0.61 and 0.92 for the $C_{\mathrm{p}}$ and refractive index, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BO with calibration ready for testing.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to validate BO performance against baselines.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BO can detect all polymers in target regions within 20-30 cycles, outperforming Fix-GP and Random.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run BO on illustrative example with three target regions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BO detected all polymers in three target regions within 20-30 cycles, outperforming Fix-GP and Random.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply multi-objective BO to optical polymer design.

evidence.quote_or_span: BO detected all polymers in the three target regions within 20–30 cycles (Fig. 3b), demonstrating a clear advantage over Fix-GP and Random.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Multi-objective BO ready for optical polymer design.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can BO discover polymers beyond the empirical Pareto frontier for refractive index and Abbe number?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multi-objective BO with EHVI can cross the empirical Pareto frontier.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run multi-objective BO for 20 cycles targeting high refractive index and Abbe number.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Designed polymers gradually approached and crossed the empirical Pareto frontier; 64% of all polymers crossed the empirical limit line.

evidence.quote_or_span: During 20 cycles of the multi-objective BO, the designed polymers gradually approached and eventually crossed the empirically known Pareto frontier
The percentage of all polymers crossing the empirical limit line was 64 %.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What chemical features contribute to high refractive index?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SPACIER autonomously learned that sulfonyl groups are promising for high refractive index.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Approximately one-quarter of top polymers contained sulfur atoms with sulfonyl groups; SPACIER autonomously learned this design principle.

evidence.quote_or_span: Approximately one-quarter of these polymers contained sulfur atoms with several sulfonyl groups ( $-SO_{2}-$ ) as substructures.
SPACIER has successfully learned this design principle autonomously.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Top candidates P1, P2, P3 selected for synthesis.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the designed polymers be synthesized and characterized?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: P1, P2, P3 can be synthesized and their properties measured.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Attempt synthesis of P1, P2, P3.
evolution_trajectory.observation: P2 synthesis failed: polymer precipitated, insoluble, no film formed; copolymerization did not yield film.

evidence.quote_or_span: During P2 synthesis, a solid was precipitated during the polymerization reaction. P2 was insoluble in commonly used solvents
the copolymer did not successfully form a film.
the synthesis of the desired polymer could be difficult because of the low nucleophilicity of the aromatic dithiol
one polymer P2, although likely synthesized, failed to form a film owing to its insolubility in organic solvents.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: P1 synthesized successfully; P3 replaced by known mpPh-PTU.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do experimental properties match calculated values?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Experimental and calculated refractive indices and Abbe numbers for P1 and mpPh-PTU are in good agreement.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure refractive index and Abbe number of P1 and compare with calculations; use literature values for mpPh-PTU.

tool_or_method.name: refractive index and Abbe number measurement

evolution_trajectory.observation: For P1: exp refractive index 1.64 vs sim 1.63; exp Abbe number 32.0 vs sim 32.0. For mpPh-PTU: exp 1.81 vs sim 1.84; exp 11.0 vs sim (not fully quoted). Good agreement.

evidence.quote_or_span: The values of the refractive indices and Abbe numbers for P1 and mpPh-PTU are in good agreement.
the experimental values were 1.64 for P1 and 1.81 for mpPh-PTU, while the calculated values were 1.63 and 1.84
the experimental values were 32.0 for P1 and 11.0 for mpPh-PTU, while the calculated values were

evolution_trajectory.state_before: RadonPy properties validated for specific cases.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Unclear if RadonPy is quantitatively predictive for other properties and polymer systems.
evolution_trajectory.observation: It remains unclear to what extent RadonPy-calculated properties are quantitatively predictive for other properties and polymer systems.

evidence.quote_or_span: it remains unclear to what extent the RadonPy-calculated properties are quantitatively predictive for other properties and polymer systems.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to avoid poorly soluble polymers in virtual library?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A machine learning solubility predictor could exclude poorly soluble polymers in advance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose integration of ML solubility predictor.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Excluding poorly soluble polymers in advance would allow construction of a high-quality virtual library.

evidence.quote_or_span: Excluding poorly soluble polymers in advance would allow the construction of a high-quality virtual library. The use of a machine learning solubility predictor can help address this issue.

validation_methods: Experimental synthesis and characterization of designed polymers (P1, mpPh-PTU).
Comparison of experimental and calculated refractive indices and Abbe numbers.
metrics: Refractive index agreement (e.g., P1: exp 1.64 vs sim 1.63).
Abbe number agreement (e.g., P1: exp 32.0 vs sim 32.0).
Percentage of polymers crossing empirical limit line (64%).
success_verification.final_conclusion: SPACIER successfully integrates automated MD with BO to design polymers with desired optical properties, autonomously learning design principles like sulfonyl groups. Experimental validation confirmed good agreement for P1, but synthesis of P2 failed due to insolubility. Limitations include restricted structural diversity and unclear generalizability of RadonPy predictions.
limitations: Only a few optical polymers significantly exceeded the empirical boundary due to limited structural diversity from one-step synthesis restriction.
Unclear to what extent RadonPy-calculated properties are quantitatively predictive for other properties and polymer systems.
Synthesis failure of P2 due to insolubility.
